page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU physics tests all scoring in the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with uncached large-block reads reaching 959.79 GB/sec and writes at 435.96 GB/sec. The instance also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation, database operations, and static web serving throughput, which reached 165.94 GB/sec. Single-core performance, however, sits in the average tier across stress-ng and Geekbench tests.

With a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, the c8gn.48xlarge is optimized for high-throughput networking and EBS-intensive tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high core density and ARM-based architecture offer a favorable performance-per-resource profile for highly parallelized workloads. This instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, Redis key-value stores, and high-volume static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.48xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.7 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by top 10% scores in multi-threaded benchmarks, software compilation, and database operations. Its memory subsystem achieves high bandwidth, with uncached reads reaching 959.79 GB/sec. Equipped with a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is designed for network-intensive and highly parallelized workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale software compilation, high-throughput static web serving, Redis deployments, and multi-threaded computational tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.48xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized instance with 192 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, built for highly parallel workloads.

tagline: A high-density, arm64 compute-optimized platform featuring 192 dedicated cores and 300 Gbps network bandwidth for highly parallel, network-intensive workloads.

Equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
